competencies.label: Executive Function
competencies.definition: Working memory, inhibitory control, and cognitive flexibility — the mental controller that turns intent into action.
competencies.whyAIAge: When AI removes external structure, internal self-direction becomes the foundation for everything else.

ageGuidance.note: Highest leverage at 3-7. Train through whole-environment activities (dramatic play, complex sport, dance) not isolated brain-training games.

evidence.citation: Diamond & Ling (2016) Conclusions about interventions, programs, and approaches for improving executive functions. Dev. Cogn. Neurosci.
Lehto, Juujarvi, Kooistra & Pulkkinen (2003) Dimensions of executive functioning: Evidence from children. Br. J. Dev. Psychol.
Cortes Pascual, Moyano & Robres (2019) The relationship between executive functions and academic performance in primary education. Front. Psychol.

competencies.label: Metacognitive Self-Direction
competencies.definition: Knowing what you know, what you don't, and what to do about it.
competencies.whyAIAge: When the model can answer anything, knowing whether to trust your gut or check is the meta-skill that beats it.

ageGuidance.note: From 4 onward. Train via 'how do you know?' prompts and explicit 'I'm confused / let me check' modelling.

evidence.citation: Education Endowment Foundation (2018) Metacognition and Self-Regulated Learning Guidance Report.
Whitebread et al. (2009) The development of two observational tools for assessing metacognition and self-regulated learning in young children. Metacognition and Learning.

competencies.label: Long-Horizon Agency
competencies.definition: Persisting through multi-day projects with a self-set goal.
competencies.whyAIAge: AI removes friction; agency is the muscle that decides what to spend the friction-budget on.

ageGuidance.note: From 5 onward via week-long building projects, garden plots, music pieces. Sub-goals + visible progress.

evidence.citation: Duckworth & Quinn (2009) Development and validation of the Short Grit Scale. Journal of Personality Assessment.
Eskreis-Winkler et al. (2018) Using wise interventions to motivate deliberate practice. Journal of Personality and Social Psychology.

competencies.label: Embodied Mastery
competencies.definition: Fine and gross motor skills tuned by deliberate, varied practice.
competencies.whyAIAge: Cognition is grounded in the body; AI can't replace the wiring laid down by sport, music, dance, craft.

ageGuidance.note: Lifelong. Highest neuroplasticity 1-12. Music training shows clean causal EF transfer (g=.35; ≥12wk @ 3x/wk @ 20-40min).

evidence.citation: Diamond (2015) Effects of Physical Exercise on Executive Functions. Annals of Sports Medicine and Research.
Sala & Gobet (2017) Does Far Transfer Exist? Negative Evidence From Chess, Music, and Working Memory Training. Current Directions in Psychological Science.
Frontiers Psychology (2025) Music training and executive functions in children: a meta-analysis (k=46, n=3,530, g=.35).

competencies.label: Deep Knowledge Retrieval
competencies.definition: Strong, durable factual knowledge that supports flexible reasoning.
competencies.whyAIAge: Generative reasoning requires a knowledge base to ground it; you can't think with facts you don't own.

ageGuidance.note: Spaced retrieval > re-reading; varied retrieval > blocked. Begin from 3 with songs, books, naming.

evidence.citation: Karpicke & Roediger (2008) The critical importance of retrieval for learning. Science.

competencies.label: Guided Curiosity
competencies.definition: Asking better and better questions, with adult-guided depth.
competencies.whyAIAge: Prompt quality is downstream of question quality; curiosity is the input to all AI co-creation.

ageGuidance.note: From 2. Adults model 'I wonder…', protect 'why' questions, point to information sources.

evidence.citation: Engel (2011) Children's Need to Know: Curiosity in Schools. Harvard Educational Review.

competencies.label: AI Literacy & Co-Creation
competencies.definition: Working *with* AI as a collaborator: prompting, evaluating, iterating, refining.
competencies.whyAIAge: The defining workplace skill of 2036. Builds the muscle of ask + verify + decide.

ageGuidance.note: From 5 in age-appropriate sandboxes. Always parent-mediated under 8. Frame AI as a tool, never a 'friend'.

evidence.citation: OECD (2026) Digital Education Outlook.
EC + OECD + Code.org (2025) AI Literacy Framework for Primary and Secondary Education.

competencies.label: Lateral Source Evaluation
competencies.definition: Checking claims by leaving the page and triangulating across sources.
competencies.whyAIAge: Hallucinations and synthetic media make 'who said this and who else confirms it' the survival skill.

ageGuidance.note: From 7. Train via fact-check exercises and 'who else says this?' prompts.

evidence.citation: Wineburg & McGrew (2019) Lateral Reading and the Nature of Expertise. Teachers College Record.

competencies.label: Creative Generation
competencies.definition: Originating things — stories, hypotheses, music, images, plans — that didn't exist before.
competencies.whyAIAge: AI is a force multiplier for creators; in 2036, taste + originality are the irreducible human contribution.

ageGuidance.note: From 2. Open-ended materials, time pressure removed, judgement deferred.

evidence.citation: Brynjolfsson, Li & Raymond (2025) Generative AI at Work. Quarterly Journal of Economics.

competencies.label: Social Attunement
competencies.definition: Reading, anticipating, and influencing other minds — theory of mind in action.
competencies.whyAIAge: Care, persuasion, leadership — the work least exposed to AI substitution. Predicts thought/attention outcomes through age 10.

ageGuidance.note: ToM crystallises 3-5; effortful control + ToM at 6 predict thought/attention problems through 10.

evidence.citation: Wellman, Cross & Watson (2001) Meta-analysis of theory-of-mind development. Child Development.
Sci. Reports (2025) Theory of mind and effortful control at 6 predict 6-10 thought/attention problems (n=214 longitudinal).

competencies.label: Emotional Resilience
competencies.definition: Recovering from setback, regulating arousal, and persisting through uncomfortable feeling.
competencies.whyAIAge: AI compresses feedback loops; without recovery muscle, kids burn out. Parent-child neural synchrony predicts internalising trajectories over 1.5 years.

ageGuidance.note: Co-regulation 1-5; self-regulation scaffolded 5-12. Sleep is the strongest known mediator (glymphatic clearance ~10.9% of variance in cognition).

evidence.citation: Skene et al. (2022) Parent-child interactions and child outcomes: A meta-analysis.
Frontiers Hum. Neurosci. (2025) Reduced glymphatic ALPS index in ADHD linked to language delay.
ABCD cohort (2025) Sleep mediates ~10.9% of cognition outcome via perivascular-space burden (n≈6,800).

competencies.label: Ethical Judgment
competencies.definition: Distinguishing right from wrong with reasons; resisting manipulation by systems and other people.
competencies.whyAIAge: Algorithmic systems will optimise for engagement against children; ethical judgment is the firewall.

ageGuidance.note: Begin 3 with fairness games; deepen to consequentialist + deontic reasoning by 8-10.

evidence.citation: Bloom (2014) Just Babies: The Origins of Good and Evil. Crown.
